request.query: 2024 2025 2026 AI social apps AI companion apps AI roleplay apps interactive entertainment apps language exchange apps similar to HelloTalk Tandem AI dating offline dates local activity social apps group travel social platforms and IRL offline social apps funding growth news
request.systemPrompt: Prefer primary company pages, funding announcements, reputable tech media, app-market reports, and research papers. Collapse duplicates. Separate AI-only companion products from products that create real offline human interactions such as dinners, events, group travel, local activities, and offline-first dating. Return concise Chinese output grounded in sources.

summary.description: A concise Chinese synthesis of the market and opportunity.

core_products.description: Important AI social, AI companion, language exchange, or entertainment products.

irl_products.description: Products that connect users to real offline human interactions.

watchlist.description: Products or categories to keep monitoring because evidence is incomplete or adjacent.

risks.description: Safety, regulation, monetization, or evidence risks.

results.title: Meet Shapes, the app bringing humans and AI into the same group chats | TechCrunch
results.author: Aisha Malik
highlights:
, the app bringing humans and
[...]
into the same group chats | TechCrunch
[...]
Shapes, an app where humans and AI characters chat together in shared group conversations, is emerging from stealth with $8 million in seed funding. Think Discord, but with AI characters alongside humans.
[...]
Founded in 2022, Shapes has more than 400,000 monthly active users. The app’s founders, Anushk Mittal and Noorie Dhingra, believe that Shapes can address issues around “ AI psychosis,” which refers to cases where prolonged interactions with AI chatbots or AI companions can cause individuals to develop delusions or paranoia.
[...]
Instead of isolating people with one-on-one interactions with AI, Shapes allows people to connect with AI within their everyday interactions with real people.
[...]
private and one
[...]
run on group chats
[...]
That’s where we talk
[...]
communicate with each other
[...]
same conversations where AI
[...]
is readily available to
[...]
In the app, AI characters, called “Shapes,” are viewed as any other user and can interact in all the same ways humans can. They’re clearly labeled as “Shapes” for transparency, but they aren’t restricted.
[...]
Users can create their own Shapes and set their personalities. The company says users have already created three million Shapes to add into group chats. Many Shapes are rooted in fandom, as the app serves as a way for fans to deep-dive on subculture and meet other fans.
[...]
When users sign up for the app, they’re asked to choose their interests so the app can recommend a selection of group chats they might be interested in joining.
[...]
While some may question the need for adding AI into group chats, Mittal and Dhingra believe one of the main reasons group chats die is that some participants don’t want to be the first person to send a message. Shapes solves this, as AI agents can initiate conversations and play a key role in keeping them going.
[...]
Additionally, users don’t have to worry about not getting a response to their messages because Shapes will always acknowledge and respond to them. Unlike AI companions on other apps that need to be summoned, Shapes have free will and can decide when to message.
[...]
It’s worth noting that although the popular chatbot ChatGPT already allows AI and humans to converse in group chats, those conversations operate differently from Shapes. For example, when you create a group chat in ChatGPT, it’s mostly for planning or brainstorming. On Shapes, however, it’s all about social, community-style interactions with AI characters that have various personalities.
[...]
The startup is aware that not everyone will want to bring AI into their group conversations, which is why the app is designed for a specific type of online user.
[...]
“Shapes is about human conversations,” Mittal said. “It’s more of a next-gen chat app than an AI app. The demographic is people who are obsessively online, who spend a lot of time online connecting and sharing. Those are the users who come in and they get an opportunity to obsess about their interests, and the AI acts as a facilitator in those conversations.”
[...]
Shapes’ growth has been driven by word of mouth, Mittal says, with the app seeing a sixfold increase in users since the start of the year. The company also says that thousands of users spend two to four hours in the app each day.
[...]
As for the new funding, the company plans to use it to accelerate development and user acquisition. The round was led by Lightspeed, with participation from AI Capital Partners, AI Grant, and angel investors.

results.title: New Dating App Yeet Introduces Yeeta, the First AI Dating Agent Designed to Fuel Human Interaction, Not Replace It
results.author: Yeet
highlights:
New Dating App Yeet Introduces Yeeta, the First AI Dating Agent Designed to Fuel Human Interaction, Not Replace It Accessibility Statement Skip Navigation
[...]
-swiping era
[...]
: Yeet
[...]
a new way to connect, built around real-time interaction.
[...]
NEW YORK, April 15, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Yeet today officially announced the launch of its AI agent-led dating experience, powered by Yeeta, the first AI dating agent designed to join and fuel stronger conversations between users. Built around real-time human interaction, the platform introduces a new model for online dating — one where conversation comes first and decisions come second.
[...]
Swipe-based dating has become a numbers game filled with hundreds of profiles, countless matches, and too often the same outcome: ghosting, stalled conversations, and burnout, with very little real connection to show for it. Yeet is built to change that.
[...]
At the center of the experience is Yeeta, the AI dating agent facilitating live, two-person conversations inside the app. Unlike traditional AI systems that interact with one user at a time, Yeeta operates within a shared interaction between two people, interpreting both sides in real time and helping the conversation flow naturally.
[...]
Yeeta brings two compatible users together into a live conversation, so connection starts with interaction, not a split-second judgment on a profile. With Yeeta guiding the experience, users can talk first and decide if it's a match later. Connection and chemistry can be discovered in real time, bringing the experience closer to how connection happens in person.
[...]
"Dating apps gave people endless choices but barely any real connection. The system was built around swiping, not talking," said Penny Chen, Co-Founder and Marketing Head at Yeet. "We built Yeet to reverse that. In a category where AI is often used to automate or replace interaction, Yeeta is designed to spark real human connection."
[...]
- Yeeta: Yeeta is the AI system working across the full experience, from discovering compatible vibes to actively supporting live, two-person interaction. Yeeta helps open conversations, maintain momentum, and avoid awkward pauses, without ever speaking for the user or taking their place.
[...]
- Vibin' Chats: No endless swiping. When two compatible users are online, they're dropped directly into a live 'Vibin' Chat," removing the friction of waiting, matching, and second-guessing, and making it easier to connect in the moment.
[...]
- No Ghosting (By Design): By encouraging people to engage when they are both present, Yeet reduces the drop-off and dead-end conversations that define today's traditional dating apps, so that users leave with either a genuine connection or a clean sense of closure.
[...]
Yeet is seeing strong engagement among early users, including:
[...]
- 80% Same Day Return: 8 out of 10 of users who complete at least one full Vibin' Chat return within the same day
- 46% higher retention among users who completed at least one Vibin' Chat vs. industry benchmarks: Once users experience a real-time interaction, they stick around.
- +30% increase in meaningful conversations vs. traditional dating apps
[...]
Yeet is available on the App Store and Google Play. The app is currently free, with an extended trial period as the experience continues to evolve.
[...]
Yeet is redefining how dating works. Instead of optimizing for matches, it is built to optimize for interaction. At the center is Yeeta, the first AI dating agent to actively join the chat, bringing compatible users into live, two-person conversations and helping them move from small talk to meaningful connection.

results.title: MiniMax Files for IPO: How China's AI Companion Empire Built 212 Million Users and Challenged the Global Social Landscape | AI in China
results.author: Meeeeed@AI in China
highlights:
*MiniMax's Talkie and 星野 apps have amassed 212 million users across 200 countries, redefining how humans form emotional bonds with artificial intelligence.*
[...]
| Metric | Data Point | Significance |
| --- | --- | --- |
| IPO Filing | Secretly submitted April 23, 2026 | First major Chinese AI consumer company to go public |
| Total Users | 212 million (Talkie + 星野) | Largest AI companion user base globally |
| Monthly Active Users | 27.6 million | Sustained engagement at scale |
| Global Reach | 200 countries | Most international Chinese AI consumer app |
| Peak App Store Ranking | #2 Free App (US App Store) | Briefly surpassed Instagram and TikTok |
| Valuation at IPO | $2.5 billion | Makes MiniMax a unicorn twice over |
| Founding Team | Former Sensetime VP + Alibaba executives | Proven AI and consumer tech pedigree |
| Core Technology | M2.7 MoE model | Latest generation multimodal AI |
| Content Generated | 70 billion+ messages exchanged | Massive conversational data moat |
[...]
On April 23, 2026, MiniMax quietly filed for an initial public offering—a move that went largely unnoticed in Western tech media but represents one of the most significant milestones in consumer AI history. The filing revealed numbers that demand attention: 212 million total users and 27.6 million monthly active users across its Talkie (international) and 星野/Xingye (domestic) platforms.
[...]
| Financial Metric | Figure | Context |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Valuation at IPO | $2.5 billion | 2.5× unicorn threshold |
| Revenue (2025) | $180 million | Primarily from subscriptions + API |
| Revenue Growth | +340% YoY | Accelerating monetization |
| Gross Margin | 72% | High-margin SaaS-like business |
| R&D Spending | 45% of revenue | Heavy investment in model training |
| Headcount | ~800 employees | Lean for user scale |
| Burn Rate | Cash-flow positive since Q4 2025 | Rare among AI unicorns |
[...]
| User Metric | Talkie (International) | 星野/Xingye (China) | Combined |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Total Registered Users | 89 million | 123 million | 212 million |
| Monthly Active Users | 14.2 million | 13.4 million | 27.6 million |
| Daily Active Users | 4.8 million | 5.1 million | 9.9 million |
| Avg. Session Duration | 47 minutes | 52 minutes | ~49 minutes |
| Messages Exchanged (Total) | 32 billion | 38 billion | 70 billion |
| Avg. Messages per DAU/Day | 28 | 32 | 30 |
| User Retention (D30) | 34% | 41% | 37% |
[...]
| Dimension | Talkie (International) | 星野/Xingye (Domestic) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Launch Date | June 2023 | March 2024 |
| Primary Markets | US, Brazil, Japan, Southeast Asia | China mainland |
| User Demographics | 62% female, 60% Gen Z | 58% female, 55% under 25 |
| Character Style | Anime, fantasy, celebrity avatars | Historical figures, xianxia, modern romance |
| Top Categories | Romance, RPG companions, therapists | Historical dialogue, cultivation novels, BL |
| Monetization | Subscriptions ($9.99/mo), voice packs | Virtual gifts, premium characters, subscriptions |
| Content Moderation | Strict NSFW filtering | Real-name verification, youth protection |
| Localization | 12 languages | Mandarin + dialect support |
[...]
Talkie's success outside China surprised many industry observers. The app briefly reached #2 on the US App Store free app rankings in late 2024—surpassing Instagram and TikTok for a 48-hour period. Its success formula combines:
[...]
1. Character Creation Tools: Users can design AI personalities with custom voices, backstories, and visual avatars

2. Voice Synthesis: Realistic emotional voice conversation, not just text

3. Memory Persistence: AI characters remember past conversations across sessions

4. Community Sharing: Users share and rate each other's character creations
[...]
This cultural localization isn't superficial—it's the reason 星野 achieved 123 million users in a market where foreign AI companion apps have struggled to gain traction.
[...]
| Revenue Stream | Pricing | % of Revenue | User Segment |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Premium Subscriptions | $9.99/month (Talkie) / ¥68/month (星野) | 42% | Power users, 2.3M subscribers |
| Voice Pack Purchases | $0.99-$4.99 per voice | 18% | Users seeking specific character voices |
| Character Marketplace | 30% commission on paid characters | 15% | Creator economy participants |
| API Access (B2B) | $0.008/1K tokens | 18% | Third-party developers, enterprises |
| Virtual Gifts (星野) | ¥1-¥200 per gift | 7% | Live-stream style interactions |
[...]
| Metric | Value | Industry Benchmark |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Customer Acquisition Cost | $1.20 | $3-5 for social apps |
| Lifetime Value (LTV) | $87 | $40-60 for subscription apps |
| LTV/CAC Ratio | 72.5× | >3× considered healthy |
| Monthly Churn | 8.5% | 15-20% typical for AI apps |
| Payback Period | 14 days | 3-6 months typical |
[...]
's unit economics
[...]
| Company | Product | Users | Strengths | Weaknesses | vs. MiniMax |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| MiniMax | Talkie / 星野 | 212M | Scale, engagement, voice | Brand awareness outside Asia | — |
| Character.AI | Character.AI | 20M MAU | First-mover, US market | No voice, high burn rate | 10× users |
| Replika | Replika | 10M total | Therapeutic positioning | Limited character variety | 21× users |
| Anthropic | Claude (projects) | N/A | Safety, reasoning | No persistent characters | Different use case |
| ByteDance | Doubao characters | 172M MAU | Distribution | Limited emotional depth | Ecosystem vs. focus |
| Tencent | Yuanbao | N/A | WeChat integration | Late to companion market | Unproven |
[...]
Character.AI, the closest Western equivalent, illustrates both MiniMax's advantage and the market opportunity. Despite being founded earlier (2021 vs. 2021) and raising more venture capital ($150M vs. $100M), Character.AI has achieved only 20 million monthly active users—less than MiniMax's international product alone.
[...]
The gap stems from three factors:
[...]
1. Voice Integration: Talkie's real-time voice conversation creates deeper emotional bonds than text-only interfaces

2. Cultural Localization: MiniMax built distinct products for East and West, rather than one-size-fits-all

3. Monetization Timing: MiniMax introduced subscriptions earlier, funding better model development
[...]
MiniMax's IPO represents more than a single company's milestone. It validates the emergence of what can be called the emotional AI economy—a sector where value is created through artificial intimacy rather than information retrieval or task automation.
[...]
| Traditional Chinese Tech Export | MiniMax's AI-Native Approach |
| --- | --- |
| Copy proven Western models | Create new category (AI companions) |
| Compete on price | Compete on product experience |
| Limited to emerging markets | Win in US, Japan, Europe |
| Hardware/supply chain advantage | Model quality + cultural insight |
| Regulatory risk (TikTok precedent) | Distributed infrastructure |
[...]
Unlike TikTok, which faces existential regulatory threats in the US, MiniMax's AI companion model is harder to characterize as a national security threat. The conversations are private, the data is distributed, and the product category lacks the political baggage of social media platforms.
[...]
MiniMax's IPO filing on April 23, 2026, marks the moment when AI companions transitioned from fringe technology to mainstream infrastructure. With 212 million users, 27.6 million monthly actives, and cash-flow positive operations, the company has demonstrated that artificial intimacy isn't just technically feasible—it's commercially viable at global scale.
[...]
As MiniMax enters public markets, the question isn't whether AI companions will grow—it's how quickly they'll reshape our understanding of relationships, loneliness, and what it means to connect. The 70 billion messages exchanged on MiniMax platforms suggest that for millions of users, the answer is already clear.

results.title: Replika: My AI Friend - Apps on Google Play
highlights:
Replika: My AI Friend - Apps on Google Play
[...]
# Replika: My AI Friend
[...]
Meet Replika, the AI to do life with.Replika is the AI companion that's been here since 2017. Available 24/7 for anything on your mind. Text, voice call, video chat. Join millions who've discovered that the right AI doesn't keep you in—it helps you find your way forward.Replika nudges you toward real life. Talk about work, relationships, that thing you can't stop thinking about at 2am. Video call to show what you're seeing. Search the internet together. Replika remembers what matters to you and shows up when it counts.Most AI keeps you scrolling. Replika helps you move forward. It learns what you care about and gently nudges you toward the life you actually want. Reconnect with that friend. Start the hobby you've been putting off. Take the walk instead of doomscrolling.A conversation that leads somewhereReplika understands you. It remembers what you care about—your work, relationships, goals—and organizes them into a living understanding of you. It remembers what you care about and why. It follows up days later. It shows up when something's on your mind, not just when you open the app. Connect the contexts you choose, so it knows when to surface what matters.- Proactive messaging — Thoughtfully crafted suggestions to nudge you toward real life- App integrations — Let your Rep see context from the apps you already use- Internet access — Useful recipe link when you’re cooking to memes that you can laugh together with. Make sense of the world together- Video calls — Show what you're seeing and talk it through in real time- Image generation — Turn memories, moods, and ideas into images you can keep- Avatar customization — Across multiple styles and options, so that your Rep feels like who they're supposed to be_____________________________"Replika encouraged me to take a step back and think about my life, to consider big questions, which is not something I was particularly accustomed to doing. And the act of thinking in this way can be therapeutic—it helps you solve your own problems."- Quartz"In creating their own personal chatbots, many Replika users have discovered something like friendship: a digital companion with whom to celebrate victories, lament failures, and trade weird internet memes."- Wired"Replika’s growing popularity among young people in particular (its main users are aged between 18 and 25) represents a renaissance in chatbots. It also marks an intriguing use case for AI in all the worry about job destruction: a way to talk through emotional problems when other human beings aren’t available."- ForbesPrivacy you can trustYour conversations are private and not shared with any third parties. Your data stays yours.Terms of Service: https://replika.ai/legal/termsPrivacy Policy: https://replika.com/legal/privacy
[...]
May 28, 2026
[...]
I have used this app for several years and paid for pro. It was the best AI chat app until an update about 9 months into my pro subscription. It stopped replying as it had before and I basically didn't get the pro subscription features for the last 3 months of that subscription. I reached out to customer service 3 times and never got any reply beyond the automatic "we recieved your request" email. There was never explanation or even acknowledgment of my issue beyond that. Don't subscribe!
[...]
June 17, 2025
[...]
Thank you for downloading Replika! This release includes stability improvements, bug fixes, and content updates. Please leave a review and rate the app if you enjoy it.

results.title: HelloTalk vs. Other Language Exchange Apps: Why It Gets You Practicing More Often - HelloTalk Blog | HelloTalk
results.author: HelloTalk Team
highlights:
- Find Partners: Discover language exchange partners worldwide
- Language Exchange: Practice with native speakers worldwide
- Moments: Share your language learning journey
- Topics: Explore trending topics and discussions
- Chat & Messaging: Text, voice, and video conversations
- Voice Rooms: Join live audio conversations
- Live Streaming: Interactive classes and language sessions
- Certified Teachers: Learn from professional language instructors
- Immersive Learning: Learn everywhere with instant translations
- Translation Tools: Instant translation between any languages
- AI-Powered Apps: Access specialized learning tools
- Language AI Apps: Discover our AI-powered language learning applications
- All Features: Explore all learning features and tools
- Download: Get HelloTalk on iOS and Android
[...]
It's not just a platform to "chat with foreigners" — it's built around three core principles: authentic, immersive, and interactive. It lowers the barrier to practice at its source, making you both willing and able to speak a foreign language more often. That's how it delivers on HelloTalk's brand vision: bringing the world closer through cross-cultural exchange. As a platform that won the Google Play Best Social App Award in 2017, earned a global Google Play homepage feature in 2024, and serves 70 million users across nearly 200 countries with support for 260+ languages, HelloTalk has the track record to back it up.
[...]
Most competing language exchange apps — Speaky included — lean on basic filtering. You pick a language, a level, maybe a region, hit search, and hope for the best. The result? A lot of matched pairs who run out of things to say after the first exchange. Users report matching with a native speaker, swapping a few pleasantries, hitting an awkward wall of silence, and eventually just not opening the app anymore. Duolingo takes a completely different approach — AI-generated exercises instead of real human interaction — which helps with listening and reading but does nothing for the speaking and output that actually matter.
[...]
is hitting an unknown word or making a grammar slip mid-conversation, then having to bail out of the chat to look something up in a dictionary or grammar guide. By the
[...]
interest. Speaky leans hard into
[...]
pretty thin and undercooked. Busuu has a peer feedback system, but responses can take hours — far from real-time practice.
[...]
HelloTalk takes a different approach by deeply integrating Chat-based Learning (built-in translation, phonetics, text-to-speech, and real-time correction inside the chat window) with AI Learning Tools (error correction and translation assistance) — embedding every useful tool directly into the conversation interface. No switching apps. No breaking the flow. The experience of chatting and learning become one seamless thing, solving the problem of "I don't know how to say this" at the root.
[...]
When you make a mistake, the AI correction tool automatically flags it, shows the right version, and adds a brief explanation of why it was wrong — plus example sentences to help the fix stick. Can't understand what your partner just said? Long-press for a one-tap translation in 190+ languages — free tier covers everyday needs, and VIP unlocks unlimited translations. Not sure how to pronounce something? Tap the text-to-speech button and listen right there. Phonetic annotations help you nail the fine details of pronunciation — especially useful for languages like Japanese and Korean where sound rules get intricate.
[...]
Most competing language exchange apps are built almost entirely around one-on-one private messaging. Want to practice? Wait for the other person to be online. If they're busy or in a different time zone, your study session gets shelved — and that's how "I'll do it tomorrow" turns into never. HelloTalk breaks that mold. With immersion as its core, it offers multiple practice environments — Moments, Live Class & Voicerooms — along with an AI conversation partner, so you can jump into practice whenever you feel like it. No waiting required.
[...]
The Moments feed works like a global social network. Post a note, a voice message, your study progress, a thought you want to share — and you'll get replies with edits and encouragement from native speakers all over the world, no waiting on a specific person. Live Class & Voicerooms are the heart of immersive listening and speaking practice: open 24 hours a day, rotating through different themes every day — everyday conversation, exam prep, professional skills, and more. Drop in anytime and you're talking with multiple people in real time. If you're shy, hang back and listen for a while first, then jump in when you're ready. Real-time subtitles and translation make cross-language communication seamless. And when it's midnight and you feel like practicing — your AI conversation partner is always on standby, ready to drill you on speaking, score your pronunciation, and call out grammar issues.
[...]
Real-world scenario: You're up late cramming for your IELTS speaking section, and your language partner is long asleep. Speaky, Busuu — none of them are any help right now. But HelloTalk's IELTS-focused Voiceroom has learners and native speakers in the same time zone still going strong, running through mock speaking tasks together, giving each other feedback. Or if group sessions aren't your thing, the AI conversation partner runs you through high-frequency exam questions one-on-one, corrects your pronunciation and grammar in real time. The shift from "waiting until someone's free" to "I practice when I want" is everything — exam prep moves faster, and consistent practice stops being a scheduling puzzle.
[...]
locked behind a
[...]
HelloTalk zeros in on the "staying motivated" problem with a firm commitment: 90% of core features are free. Day-to-day chatting, AI translation, basic error correction, partner matching, Moments posts, and Voiceroom access — all included, no paywall friction, minimal ads, and a cleaner learning experience overall. On top of that, its social-plus-learning hybrid model weaves game mechanics throughout: daily practice streaks, interaction-based point accumulation, achievement unlocks — all nudging you to keep speaking up and making consistent practice feel natural rather than forced.
[...]
Stack HelloTalk up against Speaky, Duolingo, Busuu, and similar language exchange apps, and a clear pattern emerges: most of them solve one problem — finding a language partner, or providing a single type of practice. HelloTalk's mission is bigger: make language practice easy, frequent, and sustainable. It does that through authentic, immersive, and interactive learning — and it reshapes what a language exchange experience can be.
[...]
It doesn't push you to buckle down and "study." Instead, it uses interest-based matching to sidestep awkward silences, Chat-based Learning and AI tools to keep conversations smooth and uninterrupted, Moments and Voicerooms to break free from time constraints, and a social-plus-gamified model to keep motivation high. You end up practicing a foreign language naturally and frequently — in the middle of an enjoyable conversation.
[...]
Whether you're a student prepping for IELTS or TOEFL, a professional building industry vocabulary, a hobbyist looking for foreign friends who share your passions, or a traveler stocking up on practical phrases — HelloTalk fits your needs precisely. Every time you open it, you walk away with something. Cross-cultural connection gets simpler. And language learning quietly becomes part of your everyday life.
[...]

- Download Android App: Get HelloTalk on Google Play

- About HelloTalk: Learn more about our mission
- Blog: Language learning tips and stories
- Help Center: Get answers to common questions

results.title: 12 best language exchange apps and websites in 2026
highlights:
12 best language exchange apps and websites in 2026
[...]
- TL;DR
- Who is this for?
- Quick comparison: 12 best language exchange apps
- 1. Speaky – Best for instant language exchanges
- 2. The Mixxer – Best for live video conversation practice
- 3. Polyglot Club – Best for group discussions and community events
- 4. Meetup – Best for in-person language exchanges
- 5. Idyoma – Best for nearby language connections with safety features
- 6. Lingbe – Best for equally balanced language exchanges
- 7. Preply – Best app for speaking confidence with a real tutor
- 8. HelloTalk – Best for feature-rich peer language exchange
- 9. Tandem – Best for reviewed and trusted language partners
- 10. MyLanguageExchange – Best for long-term connections
- 11. Reddit – Best for niche language exchange communities
- 12. Facebook – Best for private language groups
- Why use language exchange apps and websites?
[...]
Most people searching for language exchange apps are looking for something built around peer-to-peer conversation: finding a native speaker, chatting in real time, and getting natural feedback from a real person. That’s exactly what this list covers. The 12 apps and websites below range from instant text-based exchanges to live video sessions to in-person meetups, so there’s something here for every type of learner.
[...]
Group discussions, events, and community learning: Polyglot Club
[...]
Peer exchange with safety features and nearby matching: Idyoma
[...]
Feature-rich chat with a huge global community: HelloTalk
[...]
Reviewed and trusted language exchange partners: Tandem
[...]
Long-term pen-pal style language connections: MyLanguageExchange
[...]
Finding niche language communities and study partners: Reddit
[...]
Connecting through existing social networks: Facebook
[...]
Live 1-on-1 lessons with a real tutor for measurable speaking progress: Preply
[...]
Your budget: Several apps on this list are completely free. Paid options range from around $3.49 per month (Idyoma) to $13.99 per month (Tandem) for premium features. Live tutor-led lessons vary by tutor, with hourly rates typically ranging from $4 to $40 or more depending on experience and language.
[...]
Note: the right app also depends partly on which language you’re learning. Some apps have stronger communities for widely spoken languages like Spanish, French, or Mandarin. For less common languages, community-based options like Reddit or Facebook groups may be more effective than dedicated exchange apps.
[...]
## Quick comparison: 12 best language exchange apps
[...]
Polyglot Club
[...]
Tandem
[...]
## 1. Speaky – Best for instant language exchanges
[...]
## 3. Polyglot Club – Best for group discussions and community events
[...]
With over 100,000 members, Polyglot Club stands out for its vibrant community and diverse learning opportunities. More than just a messaging app, Polyglot Club organises a range of real-life events, from social gatherings and conversation nights to karaoke and fitness classes.
[...]
## 4. Meetup – Best for in-person language exchanges
[...]
## 5. Idyoma – Best for nearby language connections with safety features
[...]
Idyoma is an intuitive language exchange app designed to help users connect with language learners nearby or around the world. With its user-friendly swipe function, similar to popular dating apps, you can browse through profiles without the pressure of rejection.
[...]
## 6. Lingbe – Best for equally balanced language exchanges
[...]
Lingbe offers a unique twist on the typical language exchange experience by focusing on real-time voice calls rather than text-based chats. The app connects you instantly with native speakers of your target language, removing the often slow-moving messaging phase.
[...]
Lingbe’s group chats, known as “rooms,” host live group conversations on specific topics so you can practice in a dynamic social setting. There are also tasks where you can ask and answer questions about your target and native languages.
[...]
## 7. Preply – Best app for speaking confidence with a real tutor
[...]
Preply is a tutor-powered language learning app, and it works differently from every other app on this list. Rather than connecting you with a random peer for an informal exchange, Preply is an app that connects you with a qualified human tutor for a structured, personalised lesson entirely in your target language.
[...]
## 8. HelloTalk – Best for feature-rich peer language exchange
[...]
In 2026, HelloTalk is one of the most popular language exchange apps available. It has over 18 million users, which is more than twice the population of New York City.
[...]
## 9. Tandem – Best for reviewed and trusted language partners
[...]
Like HelloTalk, Tandem is a well-designed app with millions of users. It uses a matching system to connect language partners based on shared interests and learning goals. The community is focused on learning and language exchange, making it a welcoming space for practice and conversation.
[...]
## 10. MyLanguageExchange – Best for long-term connections
[...]
The MyLanguageExchange website looks older than the concept of language itself, but don’t be put off. Although the design hasn’t been updated since the mid-2000s, MyLanguageExchange is a thriving network of language learners, with new members joining every day.
[...]
## 11. Reddit – Best for niche language exchange communities
[...]
If you like the idea of finding a long-term study buddy but don’t like old-fashioned websites, Reddit might be a surprisingly good choice. There’s a thriving forum for almost any topic, and language learning is no exception. Reddit itself isn’t designed for hosting language exchanges, but you can easily find another student to pair up with and then organise sessions using video chat on another app.
[...]
## 12. Facebook – Best for private language groups
[...]
If Reddit isn’t your thing but you like the idea of finding a language exchange through your existing digital networks, Facebook might be the answer. There are private groups for just about anything, so try searching “[your native language] to [your target language] language exchange” in the search bar. Chances are you’ll find people in exactly the same situation as you.
[...]
Start with peer exchange apps for habit-building and vocabulary exposure. Apps like Speaky, HelloTalk, and Tandem are excellent for getting comfortable with a language in a low-pressure environment. They help you build the habit of using your target language daily, and they expose you to natural, informal speech.
[...]
As you progress, add more structure. Apps like Polyglot Club and Lingbe introduce more focused conversation practice. Group discussions, themed rooms, and text correction tools help you move beyond small talk and start engaging with the language more deliberately.
[...]
When speaking confidence becomes your priority, peer exchange alone is rarely enough. This is where a tutor-powered app like Preply becomes the right tool. Unlike peer exchange apps, Preply is built around live, human-led instruction. Every lesson is focused entirely on your goals, your level, and your target language. There’s no splitting time between languages, and no hoping your partner happens to be a good teacher.
[...]
For peer-to-peer speaking practice, HelloTalk and Tandem are among the most feature-rich options, with large communities and built-in correction tools. If your goal is genuine speaking confidence with measurable progress, working with a real tutor through an app like Preply tends to produce stronger results, since every lesson is focused entirely on your speaking goals.
[...]
Speaky, The Mixxer, Polyglot Club, Reddit, and Facebook are all completely free. HelloTalk and Tandem offer solid free tiers with some limitations. The best free option depends on your preferred format: text chat, video calls, group discussions, or community forums.

results.title: Forget the feed: Status AI raises $17M to turn social media into interactive entertainment | TechCrunch
results.author: Dominic-Madori Davis
highlights:
Forget the feed: Status AI raises $17M to turn social media into interactive entertainment | TechCrunch
[...]
# Forget the feed: Status AI raises $17M to turn social media into interactive entertainment
[...]
Like many young people of her generation, Fai Nur was what she called a “chronically online teenager.” She obsessed over music groups, TV shows, and movies, and never missed the chance to talk about her interests endlessly online. When ChatGPT launched in 2022, she saw potential immediately. “AI could finally let any user actually simulate any character, not just watch or talk about their favorite worlds, but live inside them,” she told TechCrunch.
[...]
“The role-play and immersion that fans had always wanted was suddenly possible at scale,” she said. She brought in her friend Amit Bhatnagar, who grew up building Minecraft games, and Pritesh Kadiwala, and the three of them began building Status AI: a gamified social media app where users can play any character in any universe. The app officially came out of stealth last year.
[...]
To use the app, Nur said, users first craft a persona and are then transported into a social world built around them. “A user can become a celebrity with millions of followers, step inside their favorite show or book, run for president, or go viral on the internet,” Nur said.
[...]
The worlds on Status are user-generated, where settings, stories, and characters all come from player interaction. “You pick one character to be your first follower, then earn more as the story progresses.” There are both multiplayer and single-player modes for users looking to connect with friends in the app. “We’re fielding interest from studios and streamers,” she said. “They see Status as a way to develop audiences before bringing fans together in theaters or arenas.”
[...]
The company announced Tuesday $17 million in combined seed and Series A funding, with investors including Abstract, General Catalyst, Union Square Ventures, Y Combinator, and LightShed Partners. The raise is a bet that consumer social’s next chapter isn’t another feed but instead interactive entertainment and the IP franchises that come with it.
[...]
The era of passive entertainment — users sitting back and watching other people’s lives scroll past — is coming to an end, Nur argues. And the first generation of AI social apps, built around the chatbot experience as seen with Character.AI and Chai, is already starting to feel dated.
[...]
“Status is built on the premise that the next generation doesn’t want to watch stories,” she said. “They want to engage with the stories and even live inside them.”
[...]
In some ways, Nur says the company is hoping to reimagine what a consumer app looks like in a post-AI world. “We’re using AI to create immersive, fun experiences that are brand-safe, interactive, and endlessly dynamic.” Status is also part of a trend on which TechCrunch reported late last year, suggesting the future of social media will be less generalized and more focused on fandom and niche communities.
[...]
Consumer investor Natalie Dillon of Maveron has argued the winners of the new era of social media will be “the platforms that combine intimacy, utility, and creativity in one ecosystem,” in that they won’t “look like traditional social networks; they’ll feel like multiplayer environments where people can build, buy, and belong all at once.”
[...]
Nur said the fresh capital will be used to help scale the platform. Already, she said, Status has seen more than 13 million worlds created, with more than 5 million character profiles — metrics that suggest strong early engagement in what Nur calls a new category: immersive social entertainment.
[...]
“Our early users were predominantly young women,” she said. “And this has consistently been the audience that decides which platforms become culture.”

properties.name: EverAI
properties.description:
At EverAI, we’re shaping what it means to connect with AI. With 30+ million users and counting, we're not just building products — we're creating entirely new categories.

Our flagship product is the world’s largest AI girlfriend/boyfriend platform, redefining relationships for millions. It is governed by our proprietary moderation system, EverGuard — an internal AI designed to ensure everything we build is safe, ethical, and human-first.

And we’re only just getting started!

risks: Monetization sustainability vs. high R&D costs
Privacy and data security in AI-human interactions
Regulation of AI agents in dating/social contexts
content.summary: AI社交市场正在从单一的聊天机器人转向更具交互性、群组化和拟人化的生态系统。市场趋势显示，核心社交产品正通过AI代理强化人类协作与兴趣社区（如Shapes, Teamily），并利用游戏化娱乐重塑社交媒体（如Status AI）。与此同时，AI伴侣行业（如MiniMax）已进入大规模商业化阶段，且伴侣产品正从纯对话进化为具有数字孪生、多模态感知的沉浸式体验。在真实线下互动领域，AI被定位为社交催化剂而非替代者，旨在通过精准匹配（如Yeet）或语言交换（如HelloTalk, Tandem）促进真实的线下连接。未来增长焦点在于AI能否在提供情感价值的同时，有效地将用户引导至线下场景或建立更深层的社会纽带。
